name: GORM
what_it_does: Full-featured ORM for Go. GORM provides auto-migration, associations, hooks, transactions, scopes, and a builder-style query API for Go applications. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and SQL Server. The most popular Go ORM by a wide margin, used in the majority of Go backend applications that need database access with ORM convenience.
use_cases: Define agent data models as Go structs with GORM tags for automatic schema migration and CRUD operations
Build Go-based agent backends with Gin or Echo + GORM for complete REST API + database stack
Perform complex agent data queries with GORM's builder API (Where, Joins, Preload, Scopes)
Implement agent data audit trails using GORM's hooks (BeforeCreate, AfterUpdate) for automated timestamps
Run database migrations for agent data schemas automatically with GORM's AutoMigrate
not_for: Performance-critical SQL — GORM adds overhead; use sqlx or database/sql directly for high-performance queries
Complex SQL analytics — GORM's query builder doesn't handle complex CTEs or window functions elegantly
best_when: You're building a Go application that needs ORM convenience (associations, migrations, hooks) without raw SQL complexity.
avoid_when: You need raw SQL performance or complex queries — use sqlx for type-safe SQL without full ORM overhead.

auth.notes: Database auth via DSN (Data Source Name) connection string. GORM doesn't manage auth.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — limited by database

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gorm.ErrRecordNotFound for missing records. Database driver errors surface with SQL state codes. GORM errors are checked via tx.Error after operations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: FirstOrCreate is idempotent. Save updates or inserts based on primary key. Transaction support for complex idempotent operations.
known_agent_gotchas: GORM errors are not returned from fluent chain calls — always check tx.Error at end of chain, not as return value from db.Where().Find()
AutoMigrate only adds columns and indexes — it never removes columns or changes column types to prevent data loss; manual migrations needed for column removal
Soft delete is automatic when model embeds gorm.Model or has DeletedAt field — queries automatically filter deleted records; use Unscoped() to include them
Preload vs Joins: Preload runs separate queries (N+1 possible), Joins uses SQL JOIN — choose based on association size and query frequency
Zero values in conditions are ignored by GORM — db.Where(&User{Age: 0}) ignores Age=0; use map or explicit Where string for zero-value conditions
GORM's connection pool defaults (10 idle, 100 max connections) may need tuning for agent services with many goroutines — configure via db.DB().SetMaxOpenConns()

security_score_components.security_notes: Parameterized queries prevent SQL injection. DSN with credentials must use environment variables. Go's type system prevents some injection paths.
